UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS BROMAN ENTERPRISES LLC AND BILL NOTHAM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE NOT BARRED BY RES JUDICATA BECAUSE THE JURY IN THE PRIOR PROCEEDING DETERMINED ONLY THAT THE ALTERNATIONS MADE TO THE PROPERTY WERE NOT MADE TO CORRECT A HABITABILITY ISSUE; IT DID NOT DETERMINE THERE WERE NO HABITABILITY ISSUES WITH THE PREMISES. MOREOVER, THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES HABITABILITY ISSUES OUTSIDE THE PERIOD COVERED BY THE UD ACTION. CAUSE OF ACTION 4, NUISANCE, IS PROPERLY PLED AS AN INDEPENDENT CAUSE OF ACTION FROM THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. CAUSE OF ACTION 5, FOR B&P 17200 VIOLATION, PROPERLY PLEADS VIOLATIONS OF THE CALIFORNIA CODE AND THE SF ADMINISTRATIVE CODE WHICH PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A CLAIM OF UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hunter W. Sims, Esq. of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, (510-457-3468), for Broman Enterprises LLC and Bill Notham, Defendants and moving parties.
Eric L. Lifschitz, Esq. & Aaron H. Darsky, Esq. of Law Offices of Eric L. Lifschitz, (415-553-6055), for Seth Skolnick, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments, modifies the tentative ruling. Final ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs BROMAN ENTERPRISES LLC and BILL NOTHAM's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend based on the fact that Plaintiff's statement to the Court in open court that Plaintiff will not amend the complaint. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hunter W. Sims, Esq. of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, (510-457-3468), for Broman Enterprises LLC and Bill Notham, Defendants and moving parties.
Eric L. Lifschitz, Esq. & Aaron H. Darsky, Esq. of Law Offices of Eric L. Lifschitz, (415-553-6055), for Seth Skolnick, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Both sides submit to the tentative ruling. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs BROMAN ENTERPRISES LLC and BILL NOTHAM's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The allegations in the complaint are not barred by res judicata because the jury in the prior proceeding determined only that the alternations made to the property were not made to correct a habitability issue; it did not determine there were no habitability issues with the premises. Moreover, the Complaint alleges habitability issues outside the period covered by the UD action. Cause of action 4, Nuisance, is properly pled as an independent cause of action from the negligence cause of action. Cause of action 5, for B&P 17200 violation, properly pleads violations of the California Code and the SF Administrative Code which provide a basis for a claim of unfair business practices. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
